briefing.headline: Rig up a Suspending Jerkbait — finesse post-spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Suspending Jerkbait is a strong choice for post-spawn with clear water and post frontal conditions.

KVD: Even in slick calm clear conditions, a jerkbait over the top of them will draw fish from distance. 'They're going to see it and they're going to come.' Don't switch to finesse just because it's calm.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: Even in slick calm clear conditions, a jerkbait over the top of them will draw fish from distance. 'They're going to see it and they're going to come.' Don't switch to finesse just because it's c
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, dock, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: 'Overcast + wind puts bass into feeding overdrive on clear reservoirs. Target main lake windblown points and where river channel pushes close to shore. Get deep jerkbait to maximum diving depth t
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on creek channel, dock, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Winter bass move shallower than most realize — if you can reach them with crankbait, jerkbait, or slow-rolled spinnerbait in 10ft or less, power baits are first option. Fish avoid expansive flats unless warming trend pushes them to gravel po...

Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

The D Shad's extra density allows use on casting gear (15-lb fluorocarbon, medium-heavy rod) unlike most soft jerkbaits — this opens up fishing thick cover without breakoffs, and enables skip casting under docks and overhangs

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal post-spawn fish hunker down at 6-12ft near cover. Males may temporarily leave fry. Focus on the deeper side of transition areas — channel edges, deeper points, and offshore structure near spawning flats.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish follow but won't commit, extend your pause to 8-10 seconds and vary the cadence
